formal_full_experiment_rule: Do not run or promote full canonical claim experiments until each project has zero P1/P2 under this audit and project-specific pre-run gates pass.

projects.bestpaper_ready_reason: Strongest project, but still requires careful paper claim locking and full miss taxonomy to remove residual reviewer attack surface.
forbidden_claims: provider-general claim outside admitted white-box cells
projects.main_claim_allowed: structured provenance watermark over admitted white-box model cells
next_actions: Use semcodebook_causal_contribution_gate_v2 only with paired component deltas.
Add miss taxonomy table to appendix and cite it in limitations.

overfit_risk.reason: Large family/scale matrix and negative controls reduce overfit risk; residual risk is mainly overclaiming no-retry or cherry-picking recovery slices.
overfit_risk.required_guard: Keep all misses in denominator and report failure-boundary taxonomy.

remaining_p2: Finish row-level positive-miss taxonomy for all 658 headline misses before using miss-analysis as a main-paper argument.
In the paper, explicitly separate supported structural provenance recovery from unsupported natural first-sample generation.

attack_negative_control.rationale: 0/48,000 negative hits and 8 attack conditions provide strong attack/control coverage.

baseline_control_strength.rationale: Controls and baseline-role boundaries exist; official-baseline claims must stay scoped to runnable/admitted cells.

claim_boundary.rationale: Current artifacts forbid first-sample/no-retry and provider-general overclaiming.

evidence_workload.rationale: 72,000 white-box records, 10 admitted models, 5 families, and all scale buckets exceed normal main-paper workload.

innovation.rationale: Structured provenance over AST/CFG/SSA with keyed schedule and ECC is a real mechanism, not just prompting.

method_rigor.rationale: Fail-closed detector, negative controls, and generation-changing ablation are strong; no-retry natural-generation is correctly excluded.

reproducibility.rationale: Hash-bound manifests and scripts exist, but full reproduction depends on large local model/GPU availability.

statistics.rationale: Wilson intervals and denominator separation are present; row-level miss taxonomy is still not fully decisive.

projects.bestpaper_ready_reason: Method is defensible as a conservative null-audit, but effect yield is too sparse for best-paper unless the protocol contribution is framed narrowly or v3 improves sensitivity.
forbidden_claims: support/public rows as part of the 300-row main denominator
next_actions: Run v3 positive/negative controls under frozen thresholds.
Only if control sensitivity improves without FP inflation, run a fresh DeepSeek live v3 claim surface.

overfit_risk.reason: Low live yield protects against false accusation but weak positive-control sensitivity invites concerns that the method is underpowered.
overfit_risk.required_guard: Freeze v3 before rerun; do not tune thresholds on the 300 live outcomes.

remaining_p1: Effect is weak for any detection claim: live yield is 6/300 and positive-control sensitivity is 170/300.
A fresh frozen v3 control/live run is required before any claim upgrade beyond conservative null-audit.
remaining_p2: Positive-control misses should be split into canary, provenance, chronology, retrieval, budget, and payload buckets.

attack_negative_control.rationale: 0/300 false positives with row hashes is good; stronger positive-control sensitivity is still needed.

baseline_control_strength.rationale: Official/control role separation is clear and support rows are excluded from the main denominator.

claim_boundary.rationale: Artifacts correctly forbid high-recall, prevalence, or provider-accusation claims.

evidence_workload.rationale: 300 live rows plus controls is acceptable for scoped DeepSeek-only audit, not yet dominant best-paper scale.

innovation.rationale: Curator-side null-audit with transcript/hash retention is a useful protocol contribution.

method_rigor.rationale: Threshold discipline is good, but 130/300 positive-control misses need stronger causal diagnosis.

reproducibility.rationale: Raw/structured hash discipline and row manifests are strong; live API rerun remains external.

statistics.rationale: CI, FDR-sensitive language, and denominator separation are present.

projects.bestpaper_ready_reason: Single-owner claim is strong; best-paper-level generality requires fresh multi-owner live score-vector evidence.
forbidden_claims: multi-owner attribution from input package alone
next_actions: Execute fresh DeepSeek multi-owner package only after raw/structured transcript hash retention is enabled.
Promote only if per-owner TPR/FPR CI and rank/AUC separation pass.

overfit_risk.reason: Perfect single-owner results can be attacked as owner leakage or shortcut even with good current controls.
overfit_risk.required_guard: Run the 6,000-row multi-owner package and report true/wrong/null/random owner margins separately.

remaining_p1: Broad multi-owner attribution is not claim-bearing until fresh score-vector outputs exist.
Perfect 300/300 result remains vulnerable to shortcut/leakage critique without multi-owner heldout evidence.
remaining_p2: Latency/query frontier should be visible in main text, not only appendix.

attack_negative_control.rationale: 0/1,200 control failures is strong; owner-heldout and task-heldout live outputs are still missing.

baseline_control_strength.rationale: Wrong/null/abstain controls exist; multi-owner controls are input-ready but not result-bearing.

claim_boundary.rationale: Current scope is correctly single-active-owner/source-bound and DeepSeek-only.

evidence_workload.rationale: APIS-300, 1,200 controls, HardDecoy, and 900 transfer rows are substantial but still single-owner scoped.

innovation.rationale: Active-owner attribution with semantic witnesses, decoys, and transfer binding is a strong mechanism.

method_rigor.rationale: 300/300 is impressive but needs multi-owner score vectors to fully defeat leakage/shortcut critiques.

reproducibility.rationale: Receipts and hashes exist; provider rerun and owner secrets remain operational dependencies.

statistics.rationale: CI and task-cluster independence are explicit; threshold-free AUC/margin evidence should be promoted after rerun.

projects.bestpaper_ready_reason: The protocol is honest and useful, but coverage is too low until v5 increases decisive routing without unsafe-pass inflation.
forbidden_claims: visible-marker diagnostic rows as main evidence
next_actions: Produce final v5 row-level evidence with the 960 marker-hidden denominator intact.
Report coverage-risk frontier and unsafe-pass upper bound in the main paper.

overfit_risk.reason: High abstention avoids unsafe overclaiming but weakens utility; trying to force labels would create overfit/unsafe-pass risk.
overfit_risk.required_guard: Improve coverage through preregistered v5 conjunction only; preserve hard ambiguity as a valid outcome.

remaining_p1: Decisive coverage is only 81/960; the current result is a selective triage surface, not a strong audit classifier.
v5 final evidence is not claim-bearing yet, so coverage cannot be upgraded.
remaining_p2: Expert review support should stay role-based and row-confirmation based without implying signatures or identity disclosure.

attack_negative_control.rationale: Unsafe-pass tracking and marker-hidden stress are strong, but v5 must be final-row claim-bearing.

baseline_control_strength.rationale: Marker-hidden/visible boundaries and controls are explicit.

claim_boundary.rationale: Artifacts forbid safety certificate or automatic classifier claims.

evidence_workload.rationale: 320 cases and 960 marker-hidden rows are solid for a scoped DeepSeek audit.

innovation.rationale: Watermark-as-security-object audit framing is valuable and well scoped.

method_rigor.rationale: Explicit abstention is honest, but decisive coverage is too low for a strong utility claim.

reproducibility.rationale: Artifacts and role-based review support exist; final v5 rows remain blocked.

statistics.rationale: Coverage-risk CI and unsafe-pass upper bound are explicit.
